question: Domain Name System (DNS)
canonical_answer: The predecessor to DNS, a static text file that resides on a computer and is used to resolve DNS host names to IP addresses. The HOSTS file is checked before the machine sends a name resolution request to a DNS name server. The HOSTS file has no extension.

appears_in.title: Comptia A+ Core 1 Study Guide
appears_in.answer: The predecessor to DNS, a static text file that resides on a computer and is used to resolve DNS host names to IP addresses. The HOSTS file is checked before the machine sends a name resolution request to a DNS name server. The HOSTS file has no extension.

appears_in.title: Comptia Exam Objectives Security+
appears_in.answer: An attack where an organization's web address is stolen by another party. The other party changes the enrollment of another's domain name without the consent of its legitimate owner.

appears_in.title: Comptia Sec Plus Objectives
appears_in.answer:
Technique used by criminals to alter DNS records and drive users to fake sites, to committing phishing.

How - modify the host file on client's device
- man in the middle
- alter the DNS record on the server to resolve to another IP address
